summary_oneline: Public forms (is_public=True) auto-register their auth-exempt paths into navigator-auth's runtime exclude list; revoked when is_public flips off.
summary: The request asks parrot-formdesigner to expose a form's read/representation URLs (get, JSON schema, rendered formats) and its result-submission URL without authentication when a new FormSchema.is_public flag is True, and to revoke that exemption when it is turned off. The source's premise — that navigator-auth's exclude routes are a frozenset frozen at server start — is outdated for the per-app exclude list: AuthHandler already seeds app['auth_exclude_list'] as a mutable list, evaluated at request time via fnmatch by all three middlewares, and exposes add_exclude_list(path). The genuine work is therefore smaller than implied on the middleware side (add a symmetric remove_exclude_list) but larger on the formdesigner side, because every form route is individually wrapped with is_authenticated, a handler-level decorator that ignores the exclude list and will 401 anonymous callers regardless. A complete solution must act at BOTH layers and re-hydrate the in-memory exclude list on startup from persisted public forms.

localization.role: seeds + mutates + request-time-evaluates the per-app exclude list
per-middleware (frozen tuple) exemption matcher via fnmatch
app key + default seed for the exclude list
handler-level auth gate that ignores the exclude list (hard blocker)
second middleware reading the same app exclude list
mounts form routes, blanket-wraps each with is_authenticated
canonical form model; lacks is_public field
lifecycle hook points that persist via registry.register

constraints.text: The per-app exclude list app['auth_exclude_list'] is already a mutable list evaluated at request time by all three middlewares (auth, abac, backends/abstract) via fnmatch — runtime add/remove is supported by design; no frozenset exists.
constraints.implication: formdesigner should target the per-app list (via app['auth'].add_exclude_list), NOT the per-middleware exclude_routes tuple, which is genuinely frozen at init.

constraints.text: navigator-auth has add_exclude_list(path) but NO removal method.
constraints.implication: A symmetric remove_exclude_list(path) (idempotent) must be added to AuthHandler to support is_public=False.

constraints.text: is_authenticated is a per-handler decorator that checks only request['authenticated'] and 401s otherwise; it does not consult the exclude list or allow_anonymous.
constraints.implication: Exempting a path in the middleware exclude list is necessary but NOT sufficient — formdesigner's _wrap_auth must be conditionally skipped/relaxed for public-form routes, or navigator-auth's is_authenticated must learn to honor the exclude list.

constraints.text: The exclude list is in-memory, re-seeded from defaults on every AuthHandler.setup().
constraints.implication: Runtime-registered public-form paths are LOST on server restart; the solution must re-hydrate them on startup from persisted forms whose is_public=True.

constraints.text: All mutating form handlers funnel through registry.register(form, overwrite=True), and registry.get returns the prior form.
constraints.implication: Centralize the add/remove toggle (e.g. in FormRegistry.register or a PublicFormGateway) and diff old-vs-new is_public rather than duplicating logic across 4 handlers.
The render endpoint's {format} segment collapses to one pattern /api/v1/forms/<id>/render/* ; other public paths are exact strings.

recent_history.author: Jesus Lara
recent_history.message: wip: new pbac implementation — introduced the mutable per-app exclude list ('avoids global mutation')

whats_new: A public-path computation helper (form_id + base_path -> list of exempt patterns)
A re-hydration hook on app startup that registers exempt paths for all persisted is_public forms
A handler-level mechanism to allow anonymous access to public-form routes (the design fork — see open questions)
what_changes: setup_form_api / _wrap_auth: public-capable routes need a conditional auth gate
create_form/update_form/patch_form/publish_form (or registry.register): toggle exclude paths on is_public transitions
non_goals: Changing the per-middleware exclude_routes tuple mechanism (frozen by design; not the right hook)
RBAC/tenant authorization redesign — public means anonymous-readable, orthogonal to RBAC
Telegram/UI surface (setup_form_ui) — only the JSON REST surface is in scope

confidence_map.claim: navigator-auth's per-app exclude list is a runtime-mutable list evaluated at request time, not a frozenset
confidence_map.reasoning: direct read of setup() + verify_exceptions + grep showing zero frozenset

confidence_map.claim: add_exclude_list exists; no removal method does
confidence_map.reasoning: direct read + grep for remove/discard returned none

confidence_map.claim: is_authenticated handler decorator will 401 anonymous callers even on exclude-listed paths
confidence_map.reasoning: decorator body checks only request['authenticated'] and raises HTTPUnauthorized; no exclude-list/allow_anonymous consultation

confidence_map.claim: FormSchema has no is_public field today
confidence_map.reasoning: direct read of model + package-wide grep returned zero

confidence_map.claim: The candidate public URLs are get_form, /schema, /render/*, and POST /data
confidence_map.reasoning: derived from route table + source intent; exact set (e.g. whether /validate is public) needs confirmation

confidence_map.claim: Runtime-registered exempt paths are lost on restart and need startup re-hydration
confidence_map.reasoning: setup() unconditionally re-seeds the list from defaults each boot

confidence_map.claim: The toggle should be centralized at registry.register / a gateway, not duplicated in handlers
confidence_map.reasoning: all handlers funnel through register; design recommendation, not a hard constraint

unknowns.question: Exactly which form URLs become anonymous when is_public=True?
unknowns.user_answer: All four: GET /forms/{id}, GET /forms/{id}/schema, GET /forms/{id}/render/* (glob), POST /forms/{id}/data, and POST /forms/{id}/validate.

unknowns.question: How to bypass the handler-level is_authenticated for public routes?
unknowns.user_answer: navigator-auth honors the exclude list: is_authenticated short-circuits when the path is in app['auth_exclude_list'] or request.allow_anonymous is set. Global fix for all consumers.

unknowns.question: Who re-hydrates the in-memory exclude list on restart?
plausible_answers: both: callback hook populated by formdesigner
unknowns.user_answer: navigator-auth exposes an exclude-provider callback API (e.g. add_exclude_provider(async_fn)); navigator-auth invokes all registered providers on_startup. parrot-formdesigner supplies a provider that yields the public paths of persisted is_public forms.

unknowns.question: What API should navigator-auth add beyond add_exclude_list(path)?
unknowns.user_answer: Idempotent add + remove_exclude_list, PLUS bulk register_exclusions(paths)/unregister_exclusions(paths) for atomic per-form multi-path toggles.

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence (C1-C4, C6) and the change is well-bounded across two known files per repo; the remaining items (U1-U4) are design decisions a spec can pin down with open questions rather than open architectural exploration. If the handler-level bypass (U2) is contested, escalate to /sdd-brainstorm first.
